text: Community nursing serves as a cornerstone of health promotion and disease prevention in Ecuador, particularly within rural and marginalized urban settings. This study examines the vital contributions of community nurses in the realms of health education, immunization initiatives, and chronic disease management. Findings indicate that targeted educational interventions significantly foster healthier lifestyle habits and reduce the prevalence of childhood malnutrition and gastrointestinal ailments. Furthermore, vaccination campaigns have expanded healthcare reach to historically underserved populations, while longitudinal follow-up programs have been instrumental in enhancing treatment adherence and patient quality of life. Despite these contributions, the field faces substantial hurdles, including inadequate infrastructure, resource scarcities, and limited access to professional development. To amplify the efficacy of community nursing, the study proposes the integration of specialized public policies, the adoption of digital health innovations, and the strengthening of intersectoral partnerships. By addressing these systemic barriers and leveraging identified opportunities, Ecuador can fortify its community nursing workforce and drive meaningful improvements in public health outcomes.
